untranslatable_reason: Signal requires forward-looking regime prediction (3-4 month lead time, event probability assessment, contingency plan logic, and dynamic capital deployment based on confirmation signals). These require predictive/probabilistic modeling, thesis validation tracking, and multi-phase position management that cannot be expressed as static indicator conditions. Specifically: (1) 'early warning signal of market regime change' is not defined quantitatively; (2) 'event does not materialize within 9 months' requires future outcome knowledge; (3) 'thesis invalidated' requires external event confirmation; (4) defensive allocation and opportunistic deployment require portfolio-level logic and conditional capital reallocation not supported by single-bar technical indicators.
translation_notes: This is a macro regime-change anticipation strategy rooted in Jack Schwager's defensive positioning philosophy. It requires fundamental/forward-looking regime prediction capability beyond standard technical analysis. A partial approximation using volatility expansion (atr_14) and trend confirmation (adx_14) was attempted, but the core signal—maintaining defensive positioning ahead of a predicted regime shift—cannot be faithfully expressed in the indicator rule language without access to: (1) predictive regime probability models, (2) external event calendars or thesis-tracking logic, (3) portfolio-level rebalancing rules, (4) multi-timeframe confirmation logic. Recommend implementing as a separate overlay strategy or rules engine with custom regime prediction module.
